instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Retirement planning 101: Tips for NRIs
Retirement is rightly called the golden period of one’s life. People dream of a good standard of living during their retirement years. And this is indeed achievable with the help of smart investment options and some well-structured retirement planning.
Now, when compared to a resident citizen, an NRI will have to make a few extra considerations. As an NRI, you must not only carry out your retirement planning at the earliest but do so smartly and with utmost dedication.
Here are some tips to help you in the process.
Financial planning for NRIs
Before you begin your retirement planning process, there are some essential financial planning principles you should be clear about.
Firstly, you must remember that your retirement planning will be distinct from that of others, since it is based on so many subjective factors. Just because someone you know is successfully following a particular method for their retirement plan, does not mean that such a method may be suitable for you as well. So, remember to chart your own financial path.
Fortunately, the underlying functioning of retirement planning is the same. Some vital pointers to consider, keeping in mind your NRI status, are:
Life expectancy - The difference between the age at which you plan to retire, and your life expectancy, must be considered. For instance, the life expectancy for Indian males, is 68.7 years. The larger the difference, the higher will be the amount required in your retirement years.
Inflation - The amount that suffices you for a year today may become inadequate in some years. Therefore, the rate of inflation must be considered when carrying out your retirement planning.
Location for retirement - When choosing NRI investment options for your retirement planning, one of the important factors to consider is the place where you wish to retire. You may choose to move back to India to enjoy your senior years. The finances required to sustain a good retirement lifestyle may differ, sometimes drastically, between India and your current host country. The foreign exchange rate and tax implications would also have to be considered.
Other kinds of financial security - During your senior years, you may have to bear high medical expenses, as health issues tend to intensify during this phase. Furthermore, you may also have plans to secure a financial legacy for your children/grandchildren. Dipping into your retirement fund for these causes may deplete it very soon. To avoid that, a life insurance plan with essential health riders is a must.
Retirement Planning Tips for NRIs
The above pointers can help you curate a retirement plan that covers the essentials. However, to make a plan that suits you specifically, you must follow these tips:
Plan as per your goals
Some people want a simple retirement life, where spending time with children, going for walks, and following hobbies, may take precedence. Others may want a more adventurous journey that includes travelling frequently. No matter what your retirement goals are, a lack of finances should be the last thing that stops you from pursuing them. Hence, the NRI investment options you choose must help you meet your long-term retirement goals.
Consider investing in Indian options
India has a large pool of investment options, of which are accessible to NRIs as well. Even if you may already have a steady portfolio in your host country, parking money in Indian investment options is a way to diversify your portfolio.
Government schemes, such as the National Pension Scheme, are also available to NRIs. If you have a high-risk appetite, then opting for equity stocks and equity mutual funds would be a option as well. The ideal option would be to select a retirement-oriented life insurance plan, commonly called an annuity, which offers regular pay-outs and also provides a life cover.
Do not fall into common traps
The common mistake many people make in this regard is that they delay retirement planning. Many think that retirement planning should only begin after one crosses 40 years of age. The truth is that the earlier you begin, the more your funds grow due to compounding, and the lesser you have to worry as you near retirement.
Another mistake commonly found is miscalculating the amount you will need. People tend to underestimate their retirement budget, which can lead to choosing the wrong kind of investment options. Yet another aspect to be careful about is taking a too-high-risk approach or a too-low-risk approach. A balanced approach would be ideal.
Whether your retirement is a few years away or a few decades away, the ideal time to start your financial planning for that period is now. Ensure to follow the pointers and tips mentioned above to get a head-start on your retirement journey. Additionally, besides from the pointers mentioned above, it is ideal to contact a financial advisor and understand more of the nuances of financial planning.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.

https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/nri/invest/4-tips-every-nri-needs-to-know-about-retirement-planning/articleshow/83299325.cms?from=mdr]
